679	\begin{lemma}[Valuative criterion of properness for \(\Quot\)]
680	  \label{lem:quot_valuative_criterion}
681	  
682	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 5, sub-section "Valuative Criterion for Properness"
683	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
684	  % L2510-L2542). Original reference: EGA IV(2) 2.8.1, cited there.
685	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
686	  % "The functor \(\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\) satisfies the following valuative
687	  %  criterion for properness over \(S\): given any discrete valuation ring
688	  %  \(R\) over \(S\) with quotient field \(K\), the restriction map
689	  %  \(\)\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(\spec R) \to \Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(\spec K)\(\)
690	  %  is bijective. This can be seen as follows.
691	  %  Given any coherent quotient
692	  %  \(q: E_K \to \F\) on \(X_R\) which defines an element \(\langle \F, q\rangle\)
693	  %  of \(\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(\spec K)\).
694	  %  Let \(\ov{\F}\) be the image of the composite homomorphism
695	  %  \(E_R \to j_*(E_K) \to j_*\F\) where \(j : X_K \hra X_R\) is the open
696	  %  inclusion. Let \(\ov{q} : E_R\to \ov{\F}\) be the induced surjection.
697	  %  Then we leave it to the reader to verify that
698	  %  \(\langle \F, q\rangle\) is an element of \(\Quot_{E/X/S}^{\Phi,L}(\spec R)\)
699	  %  which maps to \(\langle \F, q\rangle\), and is the unique such element.
700	  %  (Use the basic fact that being flat over a dvr
701	  %  is the same as being torsion-free.)"
702	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 5, "Valuative Criterion for Properness"
703	  (FGA Explained Ch.~5); EGA IV(2) 2.8.1.}
704	  The functor \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) satisfies the valuative criterion
705	  for properness over \(S\): for every discrete valuation ring \(R\) with
706	  fraction field \(K\) and every morphism \(\Spec R \to S\), the restriction
707	  map
708	  \[
709	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}(\Spec R)
710	    \longrightarrow
711	    \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}(\Spec K)
712	  \]
713	  is bijective.
714	\end{lemma}
715	
716	\begin{proof}
717	  
718	  Given a \(\Spec K\)-point \(\langle \mathcal{F}_K, q_K\rangle\) on \(X_K\),
719	  let \(j : X_K \hookrightarrow X_R\) be the open inclusion. Define
720	  \(\overline{\mathcal{F}}\) as the image of the composite
721	  \(E_R \to j_* E_K \to j_* \mathcal{F}_K\), with induced surjection
722	  \(\overline{q} : E_R \to \overline{\mathcal{F}}\). The sheaf
723	  \(\overline{\mathcal{F}}\) is torsion-free over \(R\) (being a subsheaf of
724	  \(j_* \mathcal{F}_K\)), hence \(R\)-flat (flatness over a DVR
725	  \(=\) torsion-freeness), and restricts on the generic fiber to
726	  \(\mathcal{F}_K\). The pair \((\overline{\mathcal{F}}, \overline{q})\) is
727	  the unique extension of \(\langle \mathcal{F}_K, q_K\rangle\) to a
728	  \(\Spec R\)-point (uniqueness: any other extension agrees on the dense
729	  open \(X_K \subset X_R\) and is torsion-free, hence equals the image
730	  construction). The Hilbert polynomial is constant on connected
731	  \(\Spec R\) by flatness.
732	\end{proof}
733	
734	\section{Cohomology and base change}
735	\label{sec:cohomology_base_change}
736	
737	The Quot construction uses cohomology-and-base-change in two places: the
738	boundedness step (\cref{lem:quot_boundedness}) and the Grassmannian
739	embedding (\cref{lem:quot_alpha_injective}). We record the relevant
740	statement as a named theorem so the Lean encoding can cite it directly.
741	
742	\begin{theorem}\leanok
743	[Flat base change of cohomology]
744	  \label{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}
745	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChangeCohomology}
746	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 02KH (lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology)
747	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L947-L970).
748	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
749	  % "Consider a cartesian diagram of schemes
750	  %  \(\)
751	  %  \xymatrix{
752	  %  X' \ar[d]_{f'} \ar[r]_{g'} & X \ar[d]^f \\
753	  %  S' \ar[r]^g & S
754	  %  }
755	  %  \(\)
756	  %  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module
757	  %  with pullback \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\).
758	  %  Assume that \(g\) is flat and that \(f\) is quasi-compact and quasi-separated.
759	  %  For any \(i \geq 0\)
760	  %  \begin{enumerate}
761	  %  \item the base change map of
762	  %  Cohomology, Lemma REF
763	  %  is an isomorphism
764	  %  \(\)
765	  %  g^*R^if_*\mathcal{F} \longrightarrow R^if'_*\mathcal{F}',
766	  %  \(\)
767	  %  \item if \(S = \Spec(A)\) and \(S' = \Spec(B)\), then
768	  %  \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')\).
769	  %  \end{enumerate}"
770	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 02KH (lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology).}
771	  Let
772	  \[
773	    \begin{array}{ccc}
774	      X' & \xrightarrow{g'} & X \\
775	      \downarrow f' &  & \downarrow f \\
776	      S' & \xrightarrow{g} & S
777	    \end{array}
778	  \]
779	  be a cartesian diagram of schemes with \(g\) flat and \(f\) quasi-compact and
780	  quasi-separated. Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent
781	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module with pull-back
782	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^* \mathcal{F}\). For every \(i \ge 0\):
783	  \begin{enumerate}
784	    \item the base-change map
785	      \(g^* R^i f_* \mathcal{F} \to R^i f'_* \mathcal{F}'\) is an isomorphism;
786	    \item if \(S = \Spec A\) and \(S' = \Spec B\), then $H^i(X, \mathcal{F})
787	      \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$.
788	  \end{enumerate}
789	\end{theorem}

791	\subsection{Project-side base-change substrate}
792	\label{subsec:quot_project_basechange}
793	
794	The Lean encoding of \cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology} (and the
795	Quot construction's iso between pushforward and tensor-pullback for the
796	universal section pattern) relies on four project-side declarations
797	introduced in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean}. These
798	are typed-sorry today: each carries a substantive Lean type that
799	encodes the intended mathematical statement, with the body deferred to

803	\begin{definition}\leanok
804	[Canonical base-change map on sheaves of modules]
805	  \label{def:quot_canonical_basechange_map}
806	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.canonicalBaseChangeMap}
807	  \uses{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}
808	  Given a cartesian square of schemes
809	  \(\square = (g' : X' \to X,\ f' : X' \to S',\ f : X \to S,\ g : S' \to S)\)
810	  with \(g \circ f' = f \circ g'\) and \(g\) flat, the
811	  \emph{canonical base-change map} on sheaves of modules is the natural
812	  transformation \(g'^* f^* \mathcal{F} \longrightarrow f'^* g^* \mathcal{F}\)
813	  arising from the universal property of pullback. Encoded as a morphism
814	  of \(\mathrm{ModuleCat}\)-valued sheaf functors on the pulled-back open
815	  cover; this is the morphism whose iso-status discharges
816	  \cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}(1) in the present
817	  finite-locally-free / quasi-coherent-of-finite-type setting.
818	\end{definition}
819	
820	\begin{theorem}\leanok
821	[Stalkwise base-change isomorphism on affines]
822	  \label{thm:quot_canonical_basechange_app_app_isIso}
823	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso}
824	  \uses{def:quot_canonical_basechange_map, thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}
825	  For every affine open \(V \subseteq S'\) and every affine open
826	  \(U \subseteq X\) lying over \(V\), the local component
827	  \((\text{canonicalBaseChangeMap}\ \square).\text{app}\ \mathcal{F}.\text{app}\ U\)
828	  of \cref{def:quot_canonical_basechange_map} is an isomorphism. Proof
829	  reduces (Stacks 02KH(2)) to the affine-base case: \(H^0\) commutes with
830	  flat extension of scalars on a quasi-compact quasi-separated affine
831	  morphism.
832	\end{theorem}
833	
834	\begin{theorem}\leanok
835	[Canonical base-change map is a sheaf isomorphism]
836	  \label{thm:quot_canonical_basechange_isIso}
837	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso}
838	  \uses{thm:quot_canonical_basechange_app_app_isIso}
839	  Globalising the stalk-wise statement
840	  \cref{thm:quot_canonical_basechange_app_app_isIso}: the natural
841	  transformation \(g'^* f^* \mathcal{F} \to f'^* g^* \mathcal{F}\) of
842	  \cref{def:quot_canonical_basechange_map} is an isomorphism of
843	  quasi-coherent sheaves of modules. This is the sheaf-level form of
844	  \cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}(1).
845	\end{theorem}

851	  \uses{def:quot_canonical_basechange_map}

853	  \(U \subseteq Y\), \(V \subseteq X\) with \(g(V) \subseteq U\), and a
854	  quasi-coherent sheaf of modules \(\mathcal{N}\) on \(Y\), the
855	  \emph{tensor-presentation iso} packages the canonical iso
856	  \(\Gamma(V,\, g^* \mathcal{N}) \;\cong\;
857	   \Gamma(V,\, \mathcal{O}_X) \otimes_{\Gamma(U,\, \mathcal{O}_Y)}
858	     \Gamma(U,\, \mathcal{N})\)

861	  pullback-app step of the Tilde-isoTop route (Stacks tag 01HQ / 01I8)
862	  to a base-change-of-scalars identity. The body factors as

865	  (Step 2, project-side typed sorry): the

1074	\section{Lean encoding}
1075	\label{sec:quot_lean_encoding}
1076	
1077	The Lean target file is the new
1078	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean}. The construction
1079	proceeds in three phases.
1080	
1081	\paragraph{Phase 1: Hilbert polynomial (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}).}
1082	On a finite-type morphism \(\pi : X \to S\) with \(S\) noetherian and a
1083	coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) on \(X\) with proper support over \(S\), the per-fiber
1084	Hilbert polynomial is encoded as a function $s \mapsto
1085	\Phi_{\mathcal{F},s} \in \mathbb{Q}[\lambda]$. Mathlib provides
1086	\texttt{CategoryTheory.PolynomialModule} and Euler-characteristic
1087	infrastructure on coherent sheaves on noetherian schemes; the polynomial
1088	property of \(m \mapsto \chi(\mathcal{F} \otimes L^{\otimes m})\) requires
1089	Snapper's Lemma, which is a project-side sub-build on top of the
1090	graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure.
1091	
1092	\paragraph{Phase 2: Grassmannian scheme (\cref{def:grassmannian_scheme},
1093	\cref{thm:grassmannian_representable}).} The Grassmannian
1094	\(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d)\) is encoded as the relative gluing of \(\binom{r}{d}\)
1095	affine charts \(U^I\) (each isomorphic to a relative affine space
1096	\(\mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_S\)) via the Pl\"ucker cocycle \(\theta_{I,J}\). The
1097	gluing backbone is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData}. The
1098	universal quotient sheaf \(\mathcal{U}\) is glued by the transition cocycle
1099	\(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\), and the determinant line bundle
1100	\(\det \mathcal{U}\) gives the Pl\"ucker closed embedding into
1101	\(\mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^d V)\). The representability statement uses
1102	\texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy} (as in
1103	\cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}) on the universal pair
1104	\((\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d), \mathcal{U})\).
1105	
1106	\paragraph{Phase 3: Quot scheme (\cref{thm:quot_representable}).}
1107	The Quot functor is constructed as a locally closed subfunctor of the
1108	Grassmannian
1109	\(\mathrm{Grass}(W \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_S} \mathrm{Sym}^r V, \Phi(r))\), via:
1110	\begin{enumerate}
1111	  \item the uniform \(m\)-regularity bound from
1112	    \cref{lem:quot_boundedness} (input: Castelnuovo--Mumford theorem,
1113	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.CastelnuovoMumford} -- project-side sub-build);
1114	  \item the Grassmannian embedding \(\alpha\) from
1115	    \cref{lem:quot_alpha_injective};
1116	  \item the flattening stratification (\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists}
1117	    of \cref{chap:Picard_FlatteningStratification}, A.2.a) applied to the
1118	    universal quotient on the Grassmannian, producing the locally closed
1119	    stratum \(T_0^\Phi\);
1120	  \item the valuative-criterion upgrade
1121	    (\cref{lem:quot_valuative_criterion}) to closed embedding, hence
1122	    projective \(S\)-scheme.
1123	\end{enumerate}
1124	The reduction \cref{lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W} from the universal
1125	case to the general case is encoded as a closed embedding of representable
1126	subfunctors via the kernel-vanishing scheme.
1127	
1128	\paragraph{Cohomology and base change.}
1129	\cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology} is the Mathlib-equivalent of
1130	Stacks tag 02KH. Mathlib carries a partial form
1131	(\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.flat\_base\_change}) for affine bases; the
1132	full \(R^i f_*\) statement is a project-side bridge if not present at the
1133	pinned commit.
1134	
1135	\paragraph{Dependencies summary.}
1136	This file directly consumes
1137	\cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec} (A.1.a, on disk) for relative-spectrum
1138	gluing infrastructure and
1139	\cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists} from
1140	\cref{chap:Picard_FlatteningStratification} (A.2.a, written this iter)
1141	for the flattening stratum. Downstream, \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}
1142	(A.2.c) consumes \cref{thm:quot_representable} via the special case
1143	\(E = \mathcal{O}_C\) on a smooth proper curve \(C/k\), giving the Hilbert
1144	scheme \(\Hilb_{C/k} = \Quot^{\Phi,\mathcal{O}_C(1)}_{\mathcal{O}_C/C/k}\)
1145	and its open subscheme \(\mathrm{Div}_{C/k}\) of relative effective divisors.
1146	
1147	\section{Out of scope}
1148	\label{sec:quot_out_of_scope}
1149	
1150	This chapter packages \emph{only} the Quot-scheme existence statement and
1151	its proof skeleton via the Grassmannian + flattening-stratification
1152	route. The following are explicitly out of scope here:
1153	\begin{itemize}
1154	  \item \textbf{Flattening stratification} (A.2.a) -- consumed-by, not
1155	    proved-here. Its blueprint home is the new chapter
1156	    \texttt{Picard\_FlatteningStratification.tex}.
1157	  \item \textbf{Hilbert scheme as a separate chapter}. The Hilbert scheme
1158	    \(\Hilb_{X/S}\) is the special case
1159	    \(\Hilb_{X/S} = \Quot_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}\) of the Quot scheme and is
1160	    introduced inline (\cref{def:quot_functor}); no separate chapter.
1161	  \item \textbf{Castelnuovo--Mumford \(m\)-regularity theorem} -- input to
1162	    \cref{lem:quot_boundedness}, not proved here. The proof lives in
1163	    Nitsure \S 2; for the Lean encoding it is a project-side sub-build.
1164	  \item \textbf{Semi-continuity and base change} (Nitsure \S 3) -- the
1165	    flat-base-change theorem of cohomology (\cref{thm:flat_base_change_cohomology})
1166	    is stated here as a lemma, but the upstream Mathlib bridge / project-side
1167	    sub-build is not constructed in this chapter.
1168	  \item \textbf{Snapper's Lemma} -- the polynomial-eventually property of
1169	    Euler characteristics, used in \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}. Cited
1170	    via Nitsure \S 1 and (textbook reference) Hartshorne III.5.2;
1171	    project-side sub-build for the Lean encoding.
1172	  \item \textbf{Identification with \(\mathbb{P}^n\) and relative
1173	    Grassmannian variants} (Nitsure \S 1, Exercises (1)--(4)) --
1174	    optional applications, not consumed by Route A.
1175	  \item \textbf{Hironaka's counterexample to representability of
1176	    \(\Hilb_{X/\mathbb{C}}\) in the non-projective case} (Nitsure \S 1,
1177	    Exercise (8)) -- pedagogical only; Route A operates entirely in the
1178	    projective setting.
1179	  \item \textbf{Group-scheme structure on \(\Pic_{C/k}\)} (A.2.c) -- the
1180	    abelian-group-scheme refinement is downstream of the Quot existence
1181	    statement; its blueprint home is
1182	    \cref{chap:Picard_FGAPicRepresentability}.
1183	  \item \textbf{Identity-component \(\Pic^0\) and abelian-variety
1184	    structure} (A.3) -- a separate Route A sub-row, fed by but not
1185	    constructed in this chapter.
1186	\end{itemize}

65	## Decisions identified
66	
67	### Decision 1: `IsBaseChange.of_equiv` vs `of_lift_unique` vs `ofEquiv`
68	
69	- **Mathlib idiom**: For "I have an explicit equiv `S ⊗[R] M ≃ₗ[S] N`
70	  and a 1-tensor intertwining property", the canonical constructor is
71	  `IsBaseChange.of_equiv` (`IsTensorProduct.lean:394`). The 1-tensor
72	  intertwining `∀ x, e (1 ⊗ₜ x) = f x` is exactly the data needed.
73	
74	  `IsBaseChange.of_lift_unique` is for the *universal-property* path:
75	  you must produce, for every target `Q`, a unique extension of every
76	  `R`-linear `M → Q` to an `S`-linear `N → Q`. That is structurally
77	  harder to discharge when the equiv is constructed concretely on the
78	  Spec side.
79	
80	  `IsBaseChange.ofEquiv` (capital E) only handles `e : M ≃ₗ[R] N`
81	  (same scalar ring) — gives `IsBaseChange R e.toLinearMap`. Inapplicable
82	  to Lane F's `R = Γ(X,V) → S = Γ(Y,U)` setup.
83	
84	- **Project's current path** (`QuotScheme.lean:672-695`): produces a
85	  `Nonempty {f : Γ(X,V) ⊗ Γ(N,V) ≃ₗ[Γ(Y,U)] Γ((pullback g).obj N, U) //
86	   ∀ x, f (1 ⊗ₜ x) = pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap g N e x}` via the
87	  named helper, then dispatches `IsBaseChange.of_equiv equiv hApp`.
88	
89	- **Gap**: identical. The project's path is exactly the Mathlib idiom.
90	
91	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** — `IsBaseChange.of_equiv` is the correct API
92	  path. The other constructors are structurally heavier or inapplicable.
93	
94	### Decision 2: `Nonempty Σ-pair` packaging idiom
95	
96	- **Mathlib idiom**: Mathlib has no `LinearEquiv.intertwines` predicate.
97	  The closest idioms are:
98	  (a) Return data + Prop as TWO declarations (a `noncomputable def`
99	      producing the equiv, a `theorem` recording the intertwining).
100	  (b) Return `∃ f : LinearEquiv ..., ∀ x, ...` (a Prop with a Type-valued
101	      witness — perfectly legal since `LinearEquiv` is a `Type`, not
102	      `Prop`).
103	  (c) Return `Nonempty {f : LinearEquiv // ∀ x, ...}` — the project's
104	      current choice. Definitionally a heavier wrapping of (b).
105	
106	- **Project's current path**: option (c). Consumer in
107	  `pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_isBaseChange` (L691) unpacks via
108	  `obtain ⟨equiv, hApp⟩ := ...` then feeds into `IsBaseChange.of_equiv`.
109	
110	- **Gap**: divergent-equivalent. (c) and (b) are interchangeable; (c)
111	  is mildly noisier (an extra `Subtype.mk` layer when constructing).
112	  Mathlib leans toward (b) for pure-existence statements (`∃ e, ...`).
113	  Once the helper body is closed, the natural endpoint is *not* (b)
114	  or (c) but (a): a `noncomputable def` for the equiv + a `simp` lemma
115	  for `(1 ⊗ₜ x) = baseMap x`. But while the body is a typed sorry,
116	  packaging as `Nonempty` (which only asserts inhabitedness, not
117	  computability) is the right hygiene.
118	
119	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** with current `Nonempty {f // ...}` packaging.
120	  Optionally consider switching to bare `∃ f, ...` for syntactic
121	  parsimony (saves ~1 LOC in consumer obtain pattern). NOT a blocker.
122

142	### Decision 4: Pin Steps 1 and 3 as separate typed sorries (refactor)
143	
144	- **Mathlib idiom**: Mathlib's pattern for substantive missing
145	  infrastructure is one named declaration per Stacks tag, so future
146	  porting-to-Mathlib lands one PR per tag.
147	
148	- **Project's current path**: All three Mathlib gaps (Steps 1, 2, 3)
149	  are conceptually independent, but only Step 2 is split out. Steps 1
150	  and 3 are buried inside `_sectionLinearEquiv`'s body sorry, making
151	  the `K=4 iters / 9→11 sorry creep / 6 helpers / PARTIAL×4` STUCK
152	  pattern (per iter-188 progress-critic) hard to break: the prover
153	  cannot make progress on `_sectionLinearEquiv` because closing it
154	  requires *three* substantive Stacks lemmas, only ONE of which has a
155	  name to put in scope.
156	
157	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. Cost: provers iterate on the bundled
158	  helper without a way to localize which Stacks gap is the residual
159	  obstruction; the typed-sorry budget keeps growing without progress.
160	
161	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — split out Step 1 and Step 3 as
162	  separately-named typed-sorry pins, parallel to `pullback_tildeIso`
163	  for Step 2. After this refactor, the body of `_sectionLinearEquiv`
164	  becomes pure compositional glue with no internal Mathlib gaps, and
165	  prover work can focus on the three named pins independently.

212	| 1. `IsBaseChange.of_equiv` API path | PROCEED (identical to Mathlib idiom) | informational |
213	| 2. `Nonempty Σ-pair` packaging | PROCEED (divergent-equivalent; bare `∃ f, ...` slightly cleaner but not a blocker) | informational |

216	| 4. Pin Steps 1 and 3 separately | ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB (refactor) | critical (unbundles the STUCK helper) |
217	
218	**Overall verdict**: **(A) STRUCTURAL OK** with a critical refactor
219	recommendation. The `IsBaseChange.of_equiv` choice is structurally
220	correct and does NOT need a pivot. The reason iter-188's helper has
221	been STUCK across 4 iters is *not* because the API path is wrong, but
222	because three independent Mathlib gaps are bundled into one named
223	helper, leaving provers without separate targets. Pinning Steps 1 and
224	3 separately (parallel to Step 2's existing `pullback_tildeIso` pin)
225	breaks the STUCK pattern and makes the `_sectionLinearEquiv` body
226	axiom-clean glue.

99	/-- The underlying map between abelian presheaves of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
100	noncomputable def Hom.mapPresheaf (φ : M ⟶ N) : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf :=
101	  (toPresheaf X).map φ
102	
103	/-- The application of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules to sections. -/
104	def Hom.app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : X.Opens) : Γ(M, U) ⟶ Γ(N, U) :=
105	  (forget₂ _ _).map (φ.val.app (.op U))
106	
107	@[simp] lemma mapPresheaf_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U) : φ.mapPresheaf.app U = φ.app U.unop := rfl
108	
109	@[simp]
110	lemma Hom.app_smul (φ : M ⟶ N) (r : Γ(X, U)) (x : Γ(M, U)) :
111	    φ.app U (r • x) = r • φ.app U x :=
112	  (φ.val.app (.op U)).hom.map_smul r x
113	
114	@[simp] lemma Hom.add_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ + ψ).app U = φ.app U + ψ.app U := rfl
115	@[simp] lemma Hom.sub_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ - ψ).app U = φ.app U - ψ.app U := rfl
116	@[simp] lemma Hom.zero_app : (0 : M ⟶ N).app U = 0 := rfl
117	@[simp] lemma Hom.id_app (M : X.Modules) : (𝟙 M :).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
118	@[simp] lemma Hom.comp_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (ψ : N ⟶ K) : (φ ≫ ψ).app U = φ.app U ≫ ψ.app U := rfl
119	
120	@[ext]
121	lemma hom_ext (f g : M ⟶ N) (H : ∀ U, f.app U = g.app U) : f = g := by
122	  apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
123	  ext U x
124	  exact congr($(H U.unop) x)
125	
126	lemma isSheaf (M : X.Modules) : M.presheaf.IsSheaf := SheafOfModules.isSheaf M
127	
128	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_obj : (toPresheaf X).obj M = M.presheaf := rfl
129	@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map : (toPresheaf X).map φ = φ.mapPresheaf := rfl
130	
131	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
132	lemma Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app {M N : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} :
133	    IsIso φ ↔ ∀ U, IsIso (φ.app U) := by
134	  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (toPresheaf X), NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
135	  simp [Opposite.op_surjective.forall]

141	  apply IsIso.eq_inv_of_hom_inv_id
142	  simp [← Hom.comp_app]

144	end Presheaf

151	def pushforward : X.Modules ⥤ Y.Modules :=

153

164	    ((pushforward f).map φ).app U = φ.app (f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

170	/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes
171	is left adjoint to the pushforward functor. -/
172	def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback f ⊣ pushforward f :=
173	  SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction _

iter-190+ body work (~30–50 LOC): `hU.toSpecΓ_fromSpec` + naturality of
`tilde.isoTop` + module-pullback-along-open-immersion-is-restriction. -/
private theorem pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict
    {Y : Scheme.{u}} (N : Y.Modules) {U : Y.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) :
    -- `Γ(Y, U)`-linear identification between the top section of the pullback
    -- (along `hU.fromSpec : Spec Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Y`) and `Γ(N, U)` itself. The
    -- module-action ring on the LHS is set up via the canonical algebra
    -- `Γ(Y, U) → Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤)`, which is the structure-sheaf
    -- equivalence on the affine scheme.

private theorem pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict
    {Y : Scheme.{u}} (N : Y.Modules) {U : Y.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) :
    -- `Γ(Y, U)`-linear identification between the top section of the pullback

    -- module-action ring on the LHS is set up via the canonical algebra
    -- `Γ(Y, U) → Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤)`, which is the structure-sheaf
    -- equivalence on the affine scheme.

      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom.toAlgebra

      Γ(N, U)) := by

    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom.toAlgebra

    Module.compHom _ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom
  -- The image of ⊤ under hU.fromSpec equals U (Stacks 01HH bridge).
  have hImg : (hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ (⊤ : (Spec Γ(Y, U)).Opens) : Y.Opens) = U := by

  -- iter-190+ body: Spec-transport via `hU.toSpecΓ_fromSpec` + naturality
  -- of `tilde.isoTop`. See `analogies/lane-f-isbasechange.md` Decision 3

letI algInst : Algebra Γ(Y, U) Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤) :=
    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom.toAlgebra
  letI modInst : Module Γ(Y, U) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) :=
    Module.compHom _ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom
  -- Step 1: Identify pullback along `hU.fromSpec` with the restriction functor.
  -- Mathlib's `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` gives this for any open immersion;
  -- `hU.fromSpec` is an open immersion by `IsAffineOpen.isOpenImmersion_fromSpec`.
  have isoSheaf : (Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N ≅ N.restrict hU.fromSpec :=
    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback hU.fromSpec).app N).symm
  -- Step 2: The image of ⊤ under hU.fromSpec equals U (Stacks 01HH-style bridge).
  have hImg : (hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ (⊤ : (Spec Γ(Y, U)).Opens) : Y.Opens) = U := by
    rw [Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange]; exact hU.opensRange_fromSpec
  -- Step 3: section-level map from the iso, then the rfl identification
  -- `Γ(N.restrict hU.fromSpec, ⊤) = Γ(N, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤)` (per
  -- `Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj`), then a presheaf restriction along the
  -- propositional equality `hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U` to land in `Γ(N, U)`.
  -- The forward map at the section level (additive).
  let fhom : Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) →+
      Γ(N, U) :=
    AddMonoidHom.comp
      (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by rw [hImg] : U = hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤)).op).hom.toAddMonoidHom
      (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤).hom.toAddMonoidHom
  -- The backward map.
  let ghom : Γ(N, U) →+
      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) :=
    AddMonoidHom.comp
      (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.inv ⊤).hom.toAddMonoidHom
      (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by rw [hImg] : hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U)).op).hom.toAddMonoidHom
  -- The package: we'll build the LinearEquiv from these, using:
  -- * additive bijection (fhom, ghom form an inverse pair),
  -- * Γ(Y, U)-linearity (via the natural action on Γ(N, U), and the
  --   compHom-action on the LHS).
  exact ⟨sorry⟩

650	private theorem pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict
651	    {Y : Scheme.{u}} (N : Y.Modules) {U : Y.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U) :
652	    -- `Γ(Y, U)`-linear identification between the top section of the pullback
653	    -- (along `hU.fromSpec : Spec Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Y`) and `Γ(N, U)` itself. The
654	    -- module-action ring on the LHS is set up via the canonical algebra
655	    -- `Γ(Y, U) → Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤)`, which is the structure-sheaf
656	    -- equivalence on the affine scheme.
657	    letI : Algebra Γ(Y, U) Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤) :=
658	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom.toAlgebra
659	    letI : Module Γ(Y, U) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) :=
660	      Module.compHom _ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom
661	    Nonempty (Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) ≃ₗ[Γ(Y, U)]
662	      Γ(N, U)) := by
663	  letI algInst : Algebra Γ(Y, U) Γ((Spec Γ(Y, U)), ⊤) :=
664	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom.toAlgebra
665	  letI modInst : Module Γ(Y, U) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) :=
666	    Module.compHom _ (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom
667	  -- Step 1: Identify pullback along `hU.fromSpec` with the restriction functor.
668	  -- Mathlib's `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` gives this for any open immersion;
669	  -- `hU.fromSpec` is an open immersion by `IsAffineOpen.isOpenImmersion_fromSpec`.
670	  have isoSheaf : (Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N ≅ N.restrict hU.fromSpec :=
671	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback hU.fromSpec).app N).symm
672	  -- Step 2: The image of ⊤ under hU.fromSpec equals U (Stacks 01HH-style bridge).
673	  have hImg : (hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ (⊤ : (Spec Γ(Y, U)).Opens) : Y.Opens) = U := by
674	    rw [Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange]; exact hU.opensRange_fromSpec
675	  -- Step 3: section-level map from the iso, then the rfl identification
676	  -- `Γ(N.restrict hU.fromSpec, ⊤) = Γ(N, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤)` (per
677	  -- `Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj`), then a presheaf restriction along the
678	  -- propositional equality `hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U` to land in `Γ(N, U)`.
679	  -- Define the additive equivalence.
680	  let toFun : Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) → Γ(N, U) := fun x =>
681	    (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op).hom ((Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤).hom x)
682	  let invFun : Γ(N, U) → Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) := fun y =>
683	    (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.inv ⊤).hom ((N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg).op).hom y)
684	  have left_inv : Function.LeftInverse invFun toFun := by
685	    intro x
686	    simp only [invFun, toFun, ← AddCommGrpCat.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp,
687	      eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_refl, op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id,
688	      AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply,
689	      ← Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app, isoSheaf.hom_inv_id, Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app]
690	  have right_inv : Function.RightInverse invFun toFun := by
691	    intro y
692	    simp only [invFun, toFun, ← AddCommGrpCat.comp_apply, ← Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app,
693	      isoSheaf.inv_hom_id, Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app,
694	      AddCommGrpCat.hom_id, AddMonoidHom.id_apply,
695	      ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_refl, op_id,
696	      CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
697	  have map_add' : ∀ x y, toFun (x + y) = toFun x + toFun y := by
698	    intro x y
699	    change (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom (N.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op))
700	      ((AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤)) (x + y)) =
701	      _ + _
702	    rw [show ((AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤)) (x + y)) =
703	      (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤)) x +
704	      (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app isoSheaf.hom ⊤)) y from
705	      AddMonoidHom.map_add _ _ _]
706	    exact AddMonoidHom.map_add _ _ _
707	  let addEq : Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N, ⊤) ≃+ Γ(N, U) :=
708	    { toFun := toFun
709	      invFun := invFun
710	      left_inv := left_inv
711	      right_inv := right_inv
712	      map_add' := map_add' }
713	  -- Upgrade to a `Γ(Y, U)`-LinearEquiv via the smul compatibility.
714	  refine ⟨addEq.toLinearEquiv ?_⟩
715	  -- Smul-compatibility:

723	  -- Step B: Apply Hom.app_smul (Γ(Spec ⊤)-linearity of the SheafOfModules iso).

726	  -- (= Γ(N, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤) by `restrict_obj`) factors through
727	  -- (hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).inv to land in the Γ(Y, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤)-action.
728	  -- This identification combined with `Scheme.Modules.map_smul` for the

732	  --   Y.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg).op : Γ(Y, U) → Γ(Y, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤)`,
733	  -- which encodes the "Spec-roundtrip identifies sections via the natural
734	  -- presheaf restriction" Mathlib gap (Stacks 01HH-style content).

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean

## Lane F Step 3 (`pullback_of_openImmersion_iso_restrict`, line 650)

### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Use `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback` to identify the
  pullback functor with the restrict functor along the open immersion
  `hU.fromSpec`. Combine with the rfl identification
  `Γ(N.restrict f, ⊤) = Γ(N, f ''ᵁ ⊤)` (`restrict_obj`) and the propositional
  `hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U` (from `Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange` +
  `IsAffineOpen.opensRange_fromSpec`) to build the section-level `≃ₗ[Γ(Y, U)]`.

- **Result:** PARTIAL — substantial progress; one residual `sorry` on the
  ring-level compatibility. The full chain that was constructed:
  1. **Sheaf-level iso:** `isoSheaf : (pullback hU.fromSpec).obj N ≅ N.restrict hU.fromSpec` via the symmetric of `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`.
  2. **Image equality:** `hImg : hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤ = U` via the chain
     `image_top_eq_opensRange + opensRange_fromSpec`.
  3. **Underlying `AddEquiv`:** Built explicitly via the four steps
     (fwd, bwd, left/right inverses, map_add). The left/right inverses
     close axiom-clean via `simp` chains involving
     `← AddCommGrpCat.comp_apply`, `← Functor.map_comp`, `← op_comp`,
     `eqToHom_trans`, `eqToHom_refl`, `op_id`, `CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id`,
     `AddCommGrpCat.hom_id`, `AddMonoidHom.id_apply`,
     `← Scheme.Modules.Hom.comp_app`, `isoSheaf.hom_inv_id` / `inv_hom_id`,
     and `Scheme.Modules.Hom.id_app`.
  4. **Upgrade to `LinearEquiv`** via `AddEquiv.toLinearEquiv` requires the
     smul-compatibility `∀ r x, addEq (r • x) = r • addEq x`.
     - The LHS smul is `Module.compHom`-based: unfolds to
       `((ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom r) • x` with the natural `Γ(Spec Γ(Y, U), ⊤)`-action.
     - Applied `Scheme.Modules.Hom.app_smul` (Γ(Spec)-linearity of the
       sheafy iso at sections) to migrate the smul through `Hom.app`.

- **Residual gap:** The substantive identity
  `Y.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg.symm).op ((hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).inv ((ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom r)) = r`
  (equivalently `(hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).inv ∘ (ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom =
   Y.presheaf.map (eqToHom hImg).op` as `Γ(Y, U) → Γ(Y, hU.fromSpec ''ᵁ ⊤)`).
  This expresses the "Spec-roundtrip identifies sections via the natural
  presheaf restriction" Stacks 01HH-style consistency. Mathlib has
  `appLE_appIso_inv` (`OpenImmersion.lean:229`) which gives a related
  identity for `appLE U V ≫ appIso.inv = Y.presheaf.map (homOfLE _).op`,
  plus `IsAffineOpen.fromSpec_app_self` and `ΓSpecIso_hom_fromSpec_app`
  which express the relationship between `hU.fromSpec.app U` and
  `(ΓSpecIso _).inv`. The two sides of the identity are equal by the
  Mathlib API (modulo `eqToHom` versus `homOfLE` orientation bookkeeping),
  but the closure requires careful manipulation that the iter-190
  prover budget could not complete axiom-clean.

- **Next step (iter-191):** The residual ring identity should be closeable
  via the chain
  ```
  hU.fromSpec.appLE U ⊤ ⟨_⟩ ≫ (hU.fromSpec.appIso ⊤).inv = Y.presheaf.map (homOfLE _).op
  ```
  (`appLE_appIso_inv`) combined with `Hom.appLE` unfolding + `fromSpec_app_self`
  to reduce `hU.fromSpec.appLE U ⊤ ⟨_⟩` to `(ΓSpecIso _).inv` (modulo trivial
  Spec-presheaf-of-eqToHom-on-⊤=⊤ shrinkage). Once the ring identity lands,
  the rest of the smul-compatibility closes via `Scheme.Modules.map_smul`.

- **HARD BAR status:** NOT MET (axiom-clean closure required).
  Significant qualitative progress: the AddEquiv is fully built and the
  remaining gap is reduced to a single ring identity.

- **Lemmas found / verified:**
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback`
    (`Modules/Sheaf.lean:371`) — the key bridge.
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj`
